pattern: Greedily convert each character to 'a' from left to right using the remaining budget k; stop as soon as the budget is exhausted.
hints: The cyclic distance from character c to 'a' is min(ord(c)-ord('a'), 26-(ord(c)-ord('a'))). This is the cheapest cost to turn c into 'a'.

Walk the string left to right. If the cost to change s[i] to 'a' is <= remaining k, do it and subtract the cost. Otherwise leave s[i] unchanged and stop modifying (all later characters are free and we want lex-smallest, so 'a' is ideal but stop spending).
Once the budget is exhausted, all remaining characters stay unchanged. Output the modified string.

edge_inputs.description: single character can be changed to a
single character, k exactly covers cyclic distance
k large enough to change every character to a
cyclic distance via wrap-around is shorter than direct
mid-string char costs too much, skip it and later chars stay

stress.description: random strings of length 100 with various k values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(0, 2000)
    return [s, k]
